description: The classifyCurrentSession function signature (REQ-EPNS-007 line 208) accepts Note | null, and the classification table (line 201) specifies 'Note (non-null)' for SaveFailedState. But the spec does not define what happens if currentNote is null when state is SaveFailedState. The caller is expected to provide non-null, but no precondition is formally stated and no error is specified. PROP-EPNS-004 (verification-architecture.md line 99) tests with 'Note' (non-null), so null-for-SaveFailedState is not covered by any proof obligation. An implementer might throw, return undefined, or silently misclassify. The spec should either add a precondition assertion ('currentNote must be non-null when status is save-failed or editing') or specify the behavior for null.

evidence.snippet: Line 201: '| save-failed | Note (non-null) | dirty |' | Lines 206-211: 'type ClassifyCurrentSession = (state: EditingSessionState, currentNote: Note | null) => CurrentSessionDecision;'
